briefing.headline: Rig up a Squarebill Crankbait — aggressive pre-spawn approach for muddy water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Squarebill Crankbait is a strong choice for pre-spawn with muddy water and pre frontal conditions.

Wheeler: During spring the prime depth zone on most lakes is 6 feet or less; the Jabber Jaw Hybrid Squarebill dives 3-5 feet which is the right zone for most fisheries across the country.

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

primaryApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: During spring the prime depth zone on most lakes is 6 feet or less; the Jabber Jaw Hybrid Squarebill dives 3-5 feet which is the right zone for most fisheries across the country.
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on grass, riprap, flat. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: KVD: For the shallow zone in cold water, use a flatside crankbait (Hardliner). Extremely tight action — bass use their lateral line to hunt in cold water and flatsides displace a ton of water. Small p
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on grass, riprap, flat. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: KVD: 'Rising water — bass push shallow and spread out. Best spring days can occur when warming water rises fast. Key on runoff areas: backs of creeks, culverts, residential yards, city street drainage. Water temp can vary 5 degrees between pockets...

Palaniuk: 'The spot had a riprap bank with a steep drop, then a trough that came up onto a hard bottom ridge, tapered back off into another trough. The trough made a horseshoe. A 40-yard section where the fish wanted to live, with a 25-foot hole t...

Robertson: Spring crankbait progression -- 1) Prespawn: slow-roll lipless on flats near channel banks in 1-6 feet; 2) Postspawn: big-bodied squarebill on tapering points near spawning flats in under 10 feet; 3) Late postspawn: deep diver following...

Wheeler: On flat fisheries bass stay in the shallow zone a long time before spawning, but on highland reservoirs they may stage in 6-10 feet before moving up; crankbait selection should match the lake type and depth zone.

briefing.depthStrategy: Pre-frontal pre-spawn bass are aggressive and moving shallow. Expect fish in the 4-8ft range on secondary points and channel swings. Falling pressure pulls fish up from staging areas — target the first breakline off spawning flats.
adjustIf: If the front stalls and conditions stabilize, dial back aggression and focus on higher-percentage structure
If you find a cleaner pocket or tributary, switch to natural presentations — fish in cleaner water see more lures
If fish are short-striking, add a long pause after deflections to let the lure suspend
